memoryArtifacts.headline: Hugh de Neville (died 1234)
memoryArtifacts.description: Hugh de Neville (died 1234) was the Chief Forester under the kings Richard I, John and Henry III of England; he was the sheriff for a number of counties. Related to a number of other royal officials a …

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

variants.name: Nevells

variants.name: Nevils

variants.name: Novel

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh de Neville was born about 1170, in Great Hallingbury, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Ralph de Neville, was 18 and his mother, Alice de Rafin, was 18. He married Beatrice de Turnham before April 1230. He died before 21 July 1234, in Great Wakering, Essex, England, and was buried in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh de Neville est né environ 1170, à Great Hallingbury, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ralph de Neville, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Alice de Rafin, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice de Turnham avant avril 1230. Il est décédé avant 21 juillet 1234, à Great Wakering, Essex, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Waltham Abbey, Essex, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Neville(이)가 대략 1170년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 핼링버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph de Neville(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Rafin(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1230년 4월 이전 Beatrice de Turnham(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1234년 7월 21일 이전 영국 에세스 그리트 웨이케링에서 사망했으며 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh de Neville родился около 1170 г. в местности грит холлингбури, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Ralph de Neville было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Rafin, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice de Turnham перед апрель 1230 г. Он умер перед 21 июля 1234 г. в местности грит веикиринг, эссекс, Англия и был похоронен в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия.
